evals.prompt: We're building an online bookstore. Customers browse books, place orders, and track deliveries. We integrate with Stripe for payments and SendGrid for email notifications. The frontend is a React SPA, backend is a Node.js API with a PostgreSQL database. Generate C4 diagrams in Structurizr DSL.
evals.expected_output: A complete workspace.dsl file with system context and container views, correct elements and relationships
expectations: Output contains a valid Structurizr workspace block with model and views sections
Model defines a person element for Customer
Model defines containers: SPA (React), API (Node.js), Database (PostgreSQL)
Model defines external software systems for Stripe and SendGrid
Views include both a systemContext and a container view
Relationships have descriptive labels beyond just 'uses'

evals.prompt: Create a C4 container diagram in Mermaid for our system: we have a mobile app (React Native), an API gateway (Kong), three microservices (User Service in Go, Order Service in Java/Spring, Inventory Service in Python/FastAPI), a PostgreSQL database for orders, MongoDB for inventory, and Redis for caching. External integrations: Twilio for SMS and Stripe for payments.
evals.expected_output: A Mermaid C4Container diagram with all containers, external systems, and relationships
expectations: Output contains a C4Container diagram block
Contains all 3 microservices as Container elements with correct technology labels
Contains data stores using ContainerDb for PostgreSQL and MongoDB
Contains external systems Twilio and Stripe as System_Ext
Uses a System_Boundary or Boundary to group containers
Relationships connect mobile app through API gateway to microservices

evals.prompt: Draw me an ASCII C4 system context diagram. Our system is a patient scheduling platform used by doctors and receptionists. It integrates with an EHR system (Epic) for medical records and uses Twilio for appointment reminders via SMS.
evals.expected_output: An ASCII system context diagram showing the scheduling platform, two user roles, and two external systems with labeled relationships
expectations: Diagram shows the Patient Scheduling Platform as the central element
Shows both user roles: Doctor and Receptionist as separate elements
Shows external systems: Epic EHR and Twilio
Relationships have descriptive labels explaining what flows between elements
